data.title: Top Analyst Upgrades Rigetti Stock (RGTI) to Buy on India's $8.4M Order
data.description:
B. Riley Securities' top analyst, Craig Ellis, upgraded Rigetti Computing (RGTI) to a Buy rating from Hold, while keeping his $35 price target (40.2% upside). He cited Rigetti's $8.4 million order from Indian firm C-DAC, which "enhances full-year estimate attainment visibility." Alongside Ellis, two other analysts reiterated their Buy ratings, highlighting Rigetti's expanding global footprint as a key growth driver. These ratings pushed RGTI shares up 5.5% on January 22.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Ellis is a five-star analyst on TipRanks, ranking #100 out of 12,040 analysts tracked. He has a 56% success rate and an impressive average return per rating of 31.60%.

India Order a Big Boost for Rigetti's Sales

On January 20, Rigetti announced that its Indian subsidiary received an $8.4 million purchase order to deliver a 108-qubit quantum computer to the Centre for Development of Advanced Computing (C-DAC), under the Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology. The system will be installed on-premises at C-DAC's Bengaluru center and is slated for deployment in the second half of 2026.

The order's value has been a big catalyst for analysts' enthusiasm since Rigetti reported total revenues of $7.49 million over the past twelve months. Ellis pointed out that the $8.4 million order represents 48% of the low-end and 36% of the high-end consensus revenue estimates for calendar year 2025.

Analysts Offer Bullish Views on RGTI

Rosenblatt Securities analyst John McPeake reiterated his Buy rating and $40 price target, implying 60.3% upside potential. He emphasized that the India order strengthens Rigetti's already robust installed base and validates sustained demand for its on-premises superconducting quantum solutions.

Additionally, Craig-Hallum analyst Richard Shannon reiterated his Buy rating, echoing similar catalysts and validating Rigetti's long-term outlook. While the India order is a major win, Rigetti faces certain risks including execution delays, funding constraints, and competitive pressure from IonQ (IONQ), IBM (IBM), and D-Wave Quantum (QBTS).

Is Rigetti a Good Stock to Buy?

On TipRanks, Rigetti Computing stock has a Strong Buy consensus rating based on nine Buys and two Hold ratings. The average Rigetti Computing price target of $39.75 implies 59.3% upside potential from current levels. Over the past year, RGTI shares have surged over 85%.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: Top Analysts Boost Seagate Stock (STX) Price Targets Ahead of Q2 Earnings — Here's Why
data.description:
Seagate Technology (STX) is receiving fresh attention from Wall Street ahead of its Q2 earnings report scheduled for January 27. Top-rated analysts at Morgan Stanley and Rosenblatt have raised their price targets on STX stock, citing higher demand for hard disk drives, stabilizing pricing, and a potential recovery in enterprise storage spending. Year-to-date, STX stock has gained 26%.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Seagate designs and manufactures data storage products and is best known for its hard disk drives and enterprise storage systems.

Morgan Stanley Sees Revenue Beat

Morgan Stanley's five-star-rated analyst Eirk Woodring raised STX price target to $372 from $337 and reiterated a Buy rating. He expects Seagate to slightly beat revenue estimates in the December quarter and deliver stronger-than-expected guidance for the March quarter. The firm also sees upside from higher gross margins, faster share buybacks, and better control over operating expenses, which could drive positive revisions to earnings estimates.

Woodring's upbeat outlook is based on continued strong demand for hard disk drives (HDDs), slightly better enterprise shipments, a favorable shift toward higher-capacity drives, and solid pricing trends.

Rosenblatt Lifts STX's Price Target to $370

Rosenblatt's top-rated analyst Kevin Cassidy lifted the STX price target to $370 from $270 and maintained a Buy rating on the stock. He said strong demand for Seagate's highest-capacity drives across all markets is helping the company lower the cost per bit for customers while still raising hard disk drive prices. Cassidy also remains positive on Seagate's HAMR (Heat-Assisted Magnetic Recording) technology, noting that it allows higher storage density and reduces costs, supporting long-term growth.

Is STX a Good Stock to Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Strong Buy consensus rating on STX stock based on 15 Buys and four Holds assigned in the past three months. Meanwhile, Seagate's average stock price target of $352.33 per share is 1.67% above the current share price.
data.author: Kirti Tak

data.title: Needham's Top Analyst Says, 'D-Wave Gains Edge via Circuits Buy'
data.description:
Needham's top analyst, Quinn Bolton,remains highly optimistic about D-Wave Quantum's (QBTS) competitive edge in the quantum computing landscape following its acquisition of Quantum Circuits (aka QCI). He reiterated his Buy rating and Street-high price target of $48, implying nearly 75% upside potential from current levels. Bolton awaits forthcoming qubit metrics and earnings results to update his model projections.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Bolton is a five-star analyst on TipRanks, ranking #39 out of 12,040 analysts tracked. He has a 63% success rate and an impressive average return per rating of 39.10%.

Here's What Happened

On January 20, D-Wave completed its acquisition of Quantum Circuits, reinforcing a dual-platform strategy that spans annealing and gate-model quantum systems. This move expands the firm's total addressable market (TAM) in quantum computing.

Bolton highlighted that QCI contributes superconducting gate-model systems with integrated error detection, which complements D-Wave's existing annealing technology. QCI's dual-rail qubit design offers fidelity comparable to ion traps, while leveraging superconducting gate speeds. This dual model allows for meaningful performance parity with leading gate-model rivals such as IBM (IBM), IonQ Inc. (IONQ), Rigetti Computing (RGTI), Google Quantum AI (GOOGL), and Quantinuum.  

Bolton anticipates QCI's dual-rail qubits, combined with D-Wave's cooling systems, to help build quantum computers with tens of thousands of qubits. He believes that this integration could reduce compatibility gaps between annealing and gate-model approaches. It could speed up building, software-hardware matching, and overall use by customers, adding to the stock's attractiveness.

Is D-Wave Stock a Good Investment?

Wall Street remains highly optimistic about D-Wave Quantum's long-term outlook. With 13 unanimous Buy ratings, QBTS stock commands a Strong Buy consensus rating on TipRanks. The average D-Wave Quantum price target of $39.75 implies 44.9% upside potential from current levels. Over the past year, QBTS stock has skyrocketed over 354%.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: 'Tesla FSD Prices to Rise,' Says Musk, as FSD Nears Unsupervised Capabilities
data.description:
Tesla (TSLA) CEO Elon Musk announced on X Thursday evening that prices for its Full Self-Driving (FSD) feature will increase as the company advances toward "unsupervised" driving capabilities. In his post, Musk wrote, "The $99/month for supervised FSD will rise as FSD's capabilities improve. The massive value jump is when you can be on your phone or sleeping for the entire ride (unsupervised FSD)."

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Musk recently also announced that the electric vehicle (EV) giant will stop offering FSD as a one-time purchase from February 14, and shift to a subscription model. Tesla's current Full Self-Driving (FSD) Supervised software operates at SAE Level 2+, handling complex maneuvers like city driving but requiring constant driver attention.

Musk Targets Full Autonomy

Musk envisions Tesla autos achieving full autonomous driving capabilities, called SAE Level 4, where the vehicle handles all driving tasks in specific conditions without a safety driver. Tesla's robotaxis currently operate in limited geofenced areas of Austin, Texas, and the San Francisco Bay Area in California, with a safety driver onboard.

Yesterday, Musk announced that some of its robotaxis in Austin have started testing rides without a "human safety monitor" onboard. If successful, this move could be a major win for Tesla, bringing it one step closer to rival Alphabet's (GOOGL) Waymo driverless taxis. A driverless robotaxi would strongly validate Tesla's unsupervised FSD capabilities.

Tesla has yet to announce a firm timeline for unsupervised FSD rollout, even though Musk has promoted the capability for years. When FSD was launched, Tesla faced multiple lawsuits and regulatory probes into the company's claims about its autonomous driving capabilities, forcing the company to add the word "Supervised." Despite the noise, Musk continues to position robotics, AI, and self-driving tech as Tesla's primary future growth engines, amid prolonged sales declines in its core EV business.

Is Tesla a Buy, Hold, or Sell Right Now?

Analysts remain sidelined amid the EV sales slump. On TipRanks, Tesla stock has a Hold consensus rating based on 12 Buys, 10 Holds, and seven Sell ratings. The average Tesla price target of $401.77 implies 10.6% downside potential from current levels. Over the past year, TSLA shares have gained 4.6%.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: TikTok Locks In U.S. Deal After Years of Political Crossfire
data.description:
After years of legal fights, political pressure, and repeated threats of a nationwide ban, TikTok has secured a path forward in the U.S. According to Bloomberg, TikTok and its Chinese parent ByteDance have finalized a long-awaited deal that shifts key parts of TikTok's U.S. operations to a new American-led structure.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The move removes the biggest overhang facing the popular video app and allows it to continue operating in the U.S. market.

TikTok Sets Up a New U.S.-Led Entity

As part of the deal, TikTok has set up a new U.S. entity backed by three managing investors: Oracle Corporation (ORCL), private equity firm Silver Lake, and Abu Dhabi-based investment company MGX. Together, the new investors will own 50% of the U.S. business, while existing ByteDance investors and ByteDance itself will hold the rest, in line with U.S. law.

TikTok CEO Shou Chew will remain in charge of the company globally and will take a seat on the new board. Adam Presser, a long-time TikTok executive, has been named CEO of the U.S. venture.

Agreement Clears Major Regulatory Hurdle

The deal brings closure to a five-year standoff driven by U.S. national security concerns. Lawmakers have argued that TikTok's Chinese ownership could pose risks to user data and content control. In 2024, Congress passed legislation that would have forced a ban unless TikTok's U.S. business was separated from ByteDance.

President Donald Trump praised the deal in a Truth Social post, thanking China's President Xi "for working with us and, ultimately, approving the Deal." He also said he hopes "that long into the future I will be remembered by those who use and love TikTok."

The deadline for a potential ban had been extended several times before the final agreement was reached.

Why This Deal Matters for Users and Creators

While the final valuation of TikTok's U.S. business remains unclear, estimates have ranged widely, reflecting its strong position in advertising, e-commerce, and live streaming. More importantly, the deal is a major win for creators, brands, and small businesses — and for the roughly 200 million U.S. users who rely on the app for entertainment, news, and income.

For now, TikTok's long-running U.S. drama appears to be over, and the app can finally focus on growth instead of survival.

Is ORCL Stock a Good Buy? 

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Moderate Buy consensus rating on ORCL stock based on 24 Buys, nine Holds, and one Sell assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average ORCL price target of $306.37 per share implies 71.94% upside potential.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: U.S. Stock Futures Steady After Greenland Relief Rally, Intel Stock Slips Post Q4
data.description:
U.S. stock futures were steady Thursday night after the main indexes rose for a second day in a row, helped by easing global tensions. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA), and the S&P 500 (SPX) Index were up 0.07%, 0.05%, and 0.12%, respectively, at 8:29 p.m. EDT on January 22.

Intel (INTC) fell in after-hours trading after giving a weaker outlook for the current quarter, which offset its strong results for the December quarter.

During the regular session, stocks moved higher as investors reacted to signs of lower trade risks and calmer global news. The Dow jumped more than 300 points, or about 0.6%. The S&P 500 also rose around 0.6%, while the Nasdaq Composite climbed 0.9%.

In addition, earnings remain in focus. SLB (SLB), Comerica Incorporated (CMA), Webster Financial Corporation (WBS), and Booz Allen Hamilton Holding Corporation (BAH) are set to report their quarterly results on Friday.

data.title: NVDA, AAPL, AVGO, AMD: Top Analyst Sees Improving Chip Demand Ahead of Earnings
data.description:
Chip-related stocks are heading into earnings season with growing optimism, as top Susquehanna analyst Christopher Rolland sees signs of a steady recovery across much of the chip sector, led once again by AI demand. It is worth noting that Rolland ranks 222 among more than 12,000 analysts tracked by TipRanks, with a 59% success rate and an average one-year return of 21.5%.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

In a new earnings preview report, Rolland said he expects "modest upside" in fourth-quarter results and first-quarter guidance. While AI remains the key growth engine, he noted that the broader semiconductor cycle is also improving. Also, lead times moved higher late in 2025, a sign that demand is picking up and early 2026 could be stronger than usual for several chipmakers.

NVDA and AVGO: AI and Data Centers Remain the Core Strength

AI continues to be the main source of strength across the sector. Rolland pointed to upbeat signals from Nvidia (NVDA), saying demand for its next-generation Blackwell and Rubin platforms remains very strong. According to the analyst, the backlog tied to these AI systems has grown well beyond earlier expectations, reinforcing confidence in continued data center spending.

Outside of GPUs, custom silicon is also playing a growing role. Google's (GOOGL) in-house AI chips remain a solid demand driver for Broadcom (AVGO), helping support networking and AI-related chip volumes.

AMD Benefits From Improving Server Demand

In servers, Susquehanna's checks were positive for Advanced Micro Devices (AMD), with signs that traditional x86 server demand is stabilizing after a weak period.

Rolland said enterprise and cloud customers are showing better buying activity than expected, which could support AMD's data center results in the near term.

Apple Supply Chain Offers a Bright Spot

On the consumer side, Susquehanna said PC builds in the fourth quarter came in better than expected, helped by manufacturers pulling demand forward ahead of higher memory prices.

In smartphones, overall shipments followed normal seasonal trends, but Apple (AAPL) stood out. Apple's iPhone shipments fell less than expected, a positive sign for its semiconductor suppliers as earnings approach.

Adding to that strength, recent data from Counterpoint Research showed that Apple was the world's top smartphone maker in 2025, with about a 20% global market share, supported by strong demand across emerging and mid-sized markets. The update comes ahead of Apple's fiscal first-quarter 2026 earnings, due on January 29.

Which AI Stock Is the Best Buy Right Now?

Turning to Wall Street, out of the four stocks mentioned above, Nvidia leads the group with about 43% upside and a Strong Buy rating. Broadcom also carries a Strong Buy with roughly 41% upside, while AMD offers more limited upside near 12%. Apple holds a Moderate Buy, with analysts seeing around 20% upside.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Is UnitedHealth Stock (UNH) a Buy Ahead of Q4 Earnings?
data.description:
UnitedHealth (UNH) is scheduled to release its fourth-quarter fiscal 2025 results before the market opens on January 27. Analysts expect the health insurance giant to post a significant drop in earnings due to rising medical costs in its Medicare Advantage (MA) business, with revenues showing modest growth.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Investors will focus more on UNH's 2026 guidance than on FY2025 performance, where UNH faced allegations of accounting irregularities and margin pressure.

UNH Faces Multiple Headwinds

UNH stock has lost more than 34% over the past year due to multiple company-specific and sectoral headwinds. The insurer faces a Justice Department probe into alleged MA billing malpractices. Plus, a recent Senate investigation found that UNH is using "aggressive" tactics to boost Medicare payments. Moreover, President Trump's proposed "Great Healthcare Plan" could further pressure insurers like UNH by redirecting subsidies directly to consumers, potentially shrinking their revenue from ACA marketplace plans.

Expectations from UnitedHealth

The Street expects UNH to post a 69% year-over-year decline in earnings per share (EPS) to $2.12. Sales are forecast to reach $113.8 billion, up 13% compared to Q4FY24. It is worth noting that UnitedHealth has missed earnings in two of the past eight quarters.

According to TipRanks' Options Tool, options traders expect about a 6.31% move in either direction in UNH stock in reaction to Q4FY25 results. Notably, this implied move is lower than UNH stock's average post-earnings move (in absolute terms) of 8.84% over the past four quarters.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: 'Back To Reality,' Says 5-Star Analyst About Intel Stock
data.description:
Intel (NASDAQ:INTC) shares tumbled 12% after the company beat fourth-quarter expectations but delivered a softer-than-expected outlook for the next quarter, knocking some of the wind out of what had been a strong rally into earnings day.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

For the quarter, Intel posted $13.7 billion in revenue, down 4% year over year, but $300 million above Wall Street expectations. On the earnings front, the company reported a GAAP loss of $0.12 per share, while non-GAAP EPS of $0.15 beat consensus by about $0.07. For the full year, revenue came in at $52.9 billion, essentially flat versus 2024, with adjusted earnings of $0.42 per share.

But it was the guidance, not the quarterly results, that rattled investors. Intel forecast first-quarter 2026 revenue of $11.7 billion to $12.7 billion with an expected GAAP loss of $0.21 and breakeven non-GAAP EPS, figures that came in below what many analysts were hoping for.

Part of the market's frustration stems from ongoing supply constraints and margin pressures that Intel has flagged. Even as demand for servers and AI-linked products heats up, supply chain bottlenecks, especially around new manufacturing nodes, are still shaping the near-term picture. Gross margins are under pressure and management has been forced to temper expectations for a quick rebound, comments that contributed to the stock slide.

TD Cowen says it's time to "reality-check" the narrative

5-star analyst Joshua Buchalter, of TD Cowen, echoed what the market is now wrestling with: the ultra-bullish narrative that drove INTC higher may be overdue for a reality check. Buchalter points out that the rally "had been largely driven by 'the dream' rather than the near-term reality or fundamentals." The analyst observed that results were "largely in line" but "guidance of worse-than-seasonal declines and margins below consensus outlook disappointed," and that this likely takes the wind out of the sails for those looking for near-term upside.

Buchalter also cautioned that supply constraints flagged last quarter seem to have worsened, even as demand appears to be increasing. That dynamic suggests yield improvements and pricing dynamics may not play out as favorably in the next few quarters as bulls had anticipated. His view underlines that while the roadmap remains compelling, near-term headwinds are real and can't be ignored.

What it means for investors

For investors who piled into INTC ahead of earnings, the sell-off is a reminder that expectations must be anchored in execution, not hope. Intel's long game in AI and advanced foundry could still pay off over time, but Buchalter's message is clear: it's time to go back to reality and reassess the fundamentals before calling a comeback complete.

Accordingly, Buchalter keeps a Hold rating on INTC and sets a $50 price target, suggesting only a modest 4% upside even after the stock's sharp post-earnings pullback. (To watch Buchalter's track record, click here)

Overall, the broader Wall Street view suggests a market very much on the fence. Based on 28 analyst ratings, Intel currently carries a Hold consensus, with 7 Buys, 17 Holds, and 4 Sells. The Street's average 12-month price target sits at $46.72, implying ~14% downside from recent levels. (See INTC stock forecast)
data.author: Marty Shtrubel

data.title: Why Alibaba Stock (BABA) Is Up Over 5% Today
data.description:
Alibaba Group (BABA) shares jumped about 5% on Thursday after a Bloomberg report said the company is moving closer to a potential IPO of its AI chip unit, T-Head. The report comes as investor interest in AI chipmakers grows, especially as access to NVIDIA Corporation (NVDA) products remains limited in China.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The report said Alibaba plans to restructure T-Head into a separate business with partial employee ownership, a step often taken ahead of a listing. The company is exploring an IPO, though no timeline has been set.

Founded in 2018, T-Head designs chips for computing and storage. While it remains smaller than global leaders, it has made steady progress at home. The unit recently signed a deal to supply AI accelerators for a large data center run by China Unicom, placing its chips alongside other domestic rivals.

Why China's AI Chip Push Is Gaining Speed

AI chips are in short supply, and demand is rising fast as companies build larger data centers and AI systems. In China, this has increased the push to cut reliance on U.S. chipmakers, lifting interest in local chip developers like T-Head.

Recent listings and IPO plans by other Chinese chip firms have also drawn strong investor interest. Expectations of policy support from Beijing are adding to that momentum, even though many of these companies are still in early stages.

Beyond Alibaba, other Chinese tech giants, including Baidu (BIDU), are also developing their own chips as they look for local alternatives to Nvidia hardware, which faces export limits into China.

AI Sits at the Center of Alibaba's Strategy

Alibaba has been designing its own chips for years to support its cloud and data center business. As it pushes deeper into AI, that chip work is becoming more important, linking software, services, and the hardware needed to run them.

Alibaba is backing this strategy with heavy spending. Chief executive Eddie Wu has pledged more than $53 billion for AI and infrastructure, underlining how important this shift is to Alibaba's future.

Is Alibaba Stock a Buy Now?   

Wall Street remains constructive on the stock. Alibaba stock carries a Strong Buy consensus rating based on 14 unanimous Buy ratings over the past three months. The average BABA price target stands at $203.09, suggesting roughly 20.41% upside from current levels.
data.author: Shalu Saraf
